memberdef.argsstring: (u32 BaseAddr, unsigned int Channel, unsigned int Thread)
memberdef.name: XDmaPs_Exec_DMAKILL

briefdescription.para: Use the debug registers to kill the DMA thread.

parameterdescription.para: is Debug thread encoding. 0: DMA manager thread, 1: DMA channel.

briefdescription.para: Free a buffer of the DMA program buffer.

parameterdescription.para: the DMA program buffer to be release.

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 BaseAddr, unsigned int Channel, u32 DmaProg)
memberdef.name: XDmaPs_Exec_DMAGO

briefdescription.para: XDmaPs_Exec_DMAGO - Execute the DMAGO to start a channel.

parameterdescription.para: DMA program starting address, this should be DMA address

briefdescription.para: It's the generic Done ISR.
Allocate a buffer of the DMA program buffer from the pool.

simplesect.para: The allocated buffer, NULL if there is any error.

briefdescription.para: Construct the DMA program based on the descriptions of the DMA transfer.

detaileddescription.para: The function handles memory to memory DMA transfers. It also handles unalgined head and small amount of residue tail.

parameterdescription.para: is the icache line length, in terms of bytes. If it's zero, the performance enhancement feature will be turned off.

simplesect.para: The number of bytes for the program.

briefdescription.para: Prints the content of the buffer in bytes.

parameterdescription.para: is the length of the DMA program.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a specific XDmaPs instance such that it is ready to be used.

detaileddescription.para: The data format of the device is setup for 8 data bits, 1 stop bit, and no parity by default. The baud rate is set to a default value specified by Config->DefaultBaudRate if set, otherwise it is set to 19.2K baud. The receive FIFO threshold is set for 8 bytes. The default operating mode of the driver is polled mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDmaPs instance.
is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific XDmaPs driver.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. The caller is responsible for keeping the address mapping from EffectiveAddr to the device physical base address unchanged once this function is invoked. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping changes after this function is called. If address translation is not used, pass in the physical address instead.

briefdescription.para: Reset the DMA Manager.
Reset the specified DMA Channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the channel to be reset.

briefdescription.para: Driver fault interrupt service routine This is the one that connects the GIC.

detaileddescription.para: Driver fault interrupt service routine.

briefdescription.para: Set the done handler for a channel.
Set the fault handler for a channel.
Construction function for DMAEND instruction.

detaileddescription.para: This function fills the program buffer with the constructed instruction.

parameterdescription.para: the DMA program buffer, it's the starting address for the instruction being constructed

simplesect.para: The number of bytes for this instruction which is 1.

memberdef.argsstring: (char *DmaProg, unsigned int Cn, u32 Imm, unsigned int Ns)
memberdef.name: XDmaPs_Instr_DMAGO

briefdescription.para: Construction function for DMAGO instruction.

detaileddescription.para: This function fills the program buffer with the constructed instruction.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA program buffer, it's the starting address for the instruction being constructed
is 32-bit immediate number written to the Channel Program Counter.

is Non-secure flag. If Ns is 1, the DMA channel operates in the Non-secure state. If Ns is 0, the execution depends on the security state of the DMA manager: DMA manager is in the Secure state, DMA channel operates in the Secure state. DMA manager is in the Non-secure state, DMAC aborts.

simplesect.para: The number of bytes for this instruction which is 6.

briefdescription.para: Construction function for DMALD instruction.

detaileddescription.para: This function fills the program buffer with the constructed instruction.

parameterdescription.para: the DMA program buffer, it's the starting address for the instruction being constructed

simplesect.para: The number of bytes for this instruction which is 1.

briefdescription.para: Construction function for DMALP instruction.

detaileddescription.para: This function fills the program buffer with the constructed instruction.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA program buffer, it's the starting address for the instruction being constructed
is the Loop counter register, can either be 0 or 1.
the number of iterations, LoopInterations - 1 will be encoded in the DMALP instruction.

simplesect.para: The number of bytes for this instruction which is 2.

briefdescription.para: Construction function for DMALPEND instruction.

detaileddescription.para: This function fills the program buffer with the constructed instruction.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA program buffer, it's the starting address for the instruction being constructed
is the starting address of the loop body. It is used to calculate the bytes of backward jump.
is the Loop counter register, can either be 0 or 1.

simplesect.para: The number of bytes for this instruction which is 2.

briefdescription.para: Construction function for DMAMOV instruction.

detaileddescription.para: This function fills the program buffer with the constructed instruction.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA program buffer, it's the starting address for the instruction being constructed
is the register id, 0 for SAR, 1 for CCR, and 2 for DAR

simplesect.para: The number of bytes for this instruction which is 6.

briefdescription.para: Construction function for DMANOP instruction.

detaileddescription.para: This function fills the program buffer with the constructed instruction.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA program buffer, it's the starting address for the instruction being constructed

simplesect.para: The number of bytes for this instruction which is 1.

briefdescription.para: Construction function for DMARMB instruction.

detaileddescription.para: This function fills the program buffer with the constructed instruction.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA program buffer, it's the starting address for the instruction being constructed

simplesect.para: The number of bytes for this instruction which is 1.

briefdescription.para: Construction function for DMASEV instruction.

detaileddescription.para: This function fills the program buffer with the constructed instruction.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA program buffer, it's the starting address for the instruction being constructed
is the Event number to signal.

simplesect.para: The number of bytes for this instruction which is 2.

briefdescription.para: Construction function for DMAST instruction.

detaileddescription.para: This function fills the program buffer with the constructed instruction.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA program buffer, it's the starting address for the instruction being constructed

simplesect.para: The number of bytes for this instruction which is 1.

briefdescription.para: Construction function for DMAWMB instruction.

detaileddescription.para: This function fills the program buffer with the constructed instruction.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA program buffer, it's the starting address for the instruction being constructed

simplesect.para: The number of bytes for this instruction which is 1.

briefdescription.para: Conversion function from the endian swap size to the bit encoding of the CCR.

parameterdescription.para: is the endian swap size, in terms of bits, it could be 8, 16, 32, 64, or 128(We are using DMA assembly syntax)

simplesect.para: The endian swap size bit encoding for the CCR.

briefdescription.para: Conversion function from the burst size to the bit encoding of the CCR.

parameterdescription.para: is the burst size. It's the data width. In terms of bytes, it could be 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, or 128. It must be no larger than the bus width. (We are using DMA assembly syntax.)

briefdescription.para: Conversion function from PL330 bus transfer descriptors to CCR value.

detaileddescription.para: All the values passed to the functions are in terms of assembly languages, not in terms of the register bit encoding.

briefdescription.para: Construct a loop with only DMALD and DMAST as the body using loop counter 0.

detaileddescription.para: The function also makes sure the loop body and the lpend is in the same cache line.

parameterdescription.para: is the very start address of the DMA program. This is used to calculate whether the loop is in a cache line.
is the icache line length, in terms of bytes. If it's zero, the performance enhancement feature will be turned off.
The starting address of the loop (DMALP).
The inner loop count. Loop count - 1 will be used to initialize the loop counter.

simplesect.para: The number of bytes the loop has.

memberdef.argsstring: (char *DmaProgStart, int CacheLength, char *DmaProgLoopStart, unsigned int LoopCountOuter, unsigned int LoopCountInner)
memberdef.name: XDmaPs_ConstructNestedLoop

briefdescription.para: Construct a nested loop with only DMALD and DMAST in the inner loop body.

detaileddescription.para: It uses loop counter 1 for the outer loop and loop counter 0 for the inner loop.

parameterdescription.para: is the very start address of the DMA program. This is used to calculate whether the loop is in a cache line.
is the icache line length, in terms of bytes. If it's zero, the performance enhancement feature will be turned off.
The starting address of the loop (DMALP).
The outer loop count. Loop count - 1 will be used to initialize the loop counter.
The inner loop count. Loop count - 1 will be used to initialize the loop counter.

simplesect.para: The number byes the nested loop program has.

briefdescription.para: Generate a DMA program based for the DMA command, the buffer will be pointed by the GeneratedDmaProg field of the command.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS on success.
XST_FAILURE if it fails

briefdescription.para: Free the DMA program buffer that is pointed by the GeneratedDmaProg field of the command.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS on success. XST_FAILURE if there is any error.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDmaPs *InstPtr, unsigned int Channel, XDmaPs_Cmd *Cmd, int HoldDmaProg)
memberdef.name: XDmaPs_Start

briefdescription.para: Start a DMA command.

detaileddescription.para: The command can only be invoked when the channel is idle. The driver takes the command, generates DMA program if needed, then pass the program to DMAC to execute.

parameterdescription.para: is tag indicating whether the driver can release the allocated DMA buffer or not. If a user wants to examine the generated DMA program, the flag should be set to 1. After the DMA program is finished, a user needs to explicitly free the buffer.

briefdescription.para: Checks whether the DMA channel is active or idle.

simplesect.para: 0: if the channel is idle 1: otherwise

briefdescription.para: Driver done interrupt service routine for channel 0.

detaileddescription.para: To avoid linkage error,modify all inline functions from extern inline to static inline for IAR compiler.
We need this done ISR mainly because the driver needs to release the DMA program buffer. This is the one that connects the GIC

InstPtr
is the DMA instance.
None.
None. Driver done interrupt service routines for the channels. We need this done ISR mainly because the driver needs to release the DMA program buffer. This is the one that connects the GIC

briefdescription.para: Driver done interrupt service routine for channel 1.

detaileddescription.para: We need this done ISR mainly because the driver needs to release the DMA program buffer. This is the one that connects the GIC

briefdescription.para: Driver done interrupt service routine for channel 2.

detaileddescription.para: We need this done ISR mainly because the driver needs to release the DMA program buffer. This is the one that connects the GIC

briefdescription.para: Driver done interrupt service routine for channel 3.

detaileddescription.para: We need this done ISR mainly because the driver needs to release the DMA program buffer. This is the one that connects the GIC

briefdescription.para: Driver done interrupt service routine for channel 4.

detaileddescription.para: We need this done ISR mainly because the driver needs to release the DMA program buffer. This is the one that connects the GIC

briefdescription.para: Driver done interrupt service routine for channel 5.

detaileddescription.para: We need this done ISR mainly because the driver needs to release the DMA program buffer. This is the one that connects the GIC

briefdescription.para: Driver done interrupt service routine for channel 6.

detaileddescription.para: We need this done ISR mainly because the driver needs to release the DMA program buffer. This is the one that connects the GIC

briefdescription.para: Driver done interrupt service routine for channel 7.

detaileddescription.para: We need this done ISR mainly because the driver needs to release the DMA program buffer. This is the one that connects the GIC

briefdescription.para: Print the Dma Prog Contents.
